steps.title: Step 1 — Identify the task
steps.content: Command-of-Evidence question: pick the survey quotation that best supports the students' conclusion that the garden benefits the community by fostering connections between VOLUNTEERS and OTHER residents who weren't volunteering.

steps.title: Step 2 — Match the evidence
steps.content: The correct quote must show volunteers interacting with non-volunteer neighborhood residents. Choice B (‘"We're lucky to have a few expert gardeners living in the neighborhood. Some volunteers and I have gone to them a few times with questions, and they've been eager to help us and to learn more about the project."’) describes volunteers consulting expert gardeners who live in the neighborhood and notes the experts are eager to help and learn — a direct connection between volunteers and non-volunteer residents.

steps.title: Step 3 — Per-choice elimination
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘"Our first challenge was deciding what plants would be most suitable to the climate and soil here in Atlanta. We needed plants that could survive the hot and humid summers."’: describes a horticultural/logistical challenge; no interaction with non-volunteer residents. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘"I love getting the opportunity to be outside and around nature, especially on days when the weather is nice."’: personal benefit to the volunteer; no community connection mentioned. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘"My favorite thing about the garden is the feeling of pride I get when I walk by each day. As I see the plants growing, I feel good knowing I had a small part in creating this beautiful space in the neighborhood."’: individual emotional payoff; doesn't involve other residents. ✗
That confirms B is correct.
